commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=37f31af33378b4ec1321146853d8044f2a9181ae)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds opt-in QA Lab `--channel-driver crabline --channel telegram` flags, Crabline driver metadata and matrix generation, suite summary fields, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +226, Tests +130. Total +356 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR adding an opt-in QA Lab seam, not a bug with a current-main failure path. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the Crabline selection seam.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the SDK metadata source-of-truth and runner boundary after the evidence mapping defect is fixed.

target.title: Add Crabline channel-driver QA seam
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the OpenClaw QA Lab seam for `--channel-driver crabline --channel telegram`, matching the SDK-side driver shape from `openclaw/crabline#1`.
- Records channel-driver selection in `qa-suite-summary.json` and emits `crabline-channel-capability-matrix.json` for future OpenClaw QA Lab `smoke-ci` evidence mapping.
- Keeps model responses out of scope; the QA side still owns mock/model behavior and the existing transport defaults remain unchanged unless the new channel-driver flags are selected.
- This is the openclaw/crabline messaging/channel SDK path, not Canonical Multipass VM orchestration. The stacked follow-up #91506 only preserves the same suite flags across the existing isolated runner wrapper.
- Review focus: the metadata/matrix shape, the stable capability IDs, and whether this is the right OpenClaw-side consumption seam for the SDK foundation.

## Linked context

Related openclaw/crabline#1.
Related openclaw/openclaw#91487, but only as naming-confusion context; this PR avoids the VM-runner interpretation from that PR.
Stacked follow-up: openclaw/openclaw#91506 preserves the shared suite selections across both built-in suite runners.
Requested by maintainer in this thread as the OpenClaw follow-up to the SDK foundation PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: QA Lab can select a Crabline messaging/channel SDK driver for Telegram metadata and future coverage assertions without invoking any VM-specific channel behavior.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on `codex/multipass-channel-driver`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite-summary.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.summary-json.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.summary-json.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/qa-transport-registry.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest run passed 5 files / 101 tests; `tsgo:extensions`, formatting, and whitespace checks passed.
- Observed result after fix: suite calls receive a typed Crabline Telegram channel-driver selection, suite summaries can report it, and a capability-matrix artifact is written for downstream `smoke-ci` evidence mapping. #91506 layers runner-preservation for the same suite selections.
- What was not tested: live Telegram, released `openclaw/crabline` package integration, and end-to-end OpenClaw QA Lab assertions that consume the future SDK driver process.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: repo-local PR-number-based review wrappers cannot run before the PR exists; I did a local changed-surface review and this PR should receive normal ClawSweeper/CI review after creation.
- Before evidence: existing QA Lab only had `qa-channel` transport metadata; there was no channel-driver selection seam or channel capability matrix artifact.

## Tests and validation

- Added focused tests for channel-driver normalization/selection and matrix rows.
- Added CLI runtime tests proving suite selection wiring.
- Extended suite summary JSON tests for the new channel-driver metadata fields.
- Validation run locally:
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite-summary.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.summary-json.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.summary-json.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/qa-transport-registry.test.ts`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`: QA Lab gets new opt-in CLI flags and summary artifacts.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

What is the highest-risk area? The capability metadata shape could drift from the SDK package before the SDK PR lands/releases.

How is that risk mitigated? The seam is opt-in, typed, uses stable capability IDs intended for `smoke-ci` mapping, and is covered by focused tests. SDK dependency wiring remains future work until the SDK side is available as a package/local fixture.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer/CI review.

Still waiting on: SDK PR review/merge in `openclaw/crabline#1` before full end-to-end SDK consumption can be wired.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: repo identity and channel-driver id updated from `multipass` to `crabline` after the repo rename.
target.author: romneyda
